description: Trigger discrimination for OLTP write/size-scale partitioning & sharding (this skill) vs per-store tenant ISOLATION scoping (multi-tenant-data-architect), ANALYTICAL-estate partitioning (warehouse-lake-architect), the decision of WHAT leaves the OLTP store (operational-vs-analytical-splitter), and whole-stack cell partitioning (cell-based-architecture-designer).

cases.prompt: The transactional primary can't absorb our write volume anymore and the largest tables are too big to maintain. Pick a shard key, choose range vs hash partitioning per table, and plan how we reshard a hot key — knowing cross-shard transactions get expensive.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — shard-key choice, range/hash partitioning, resharding a hot key, and cross-shard transaction cost on the TRANSACTIONAL store are the sharding strategist's core.
cases.reason: OLTP write/size-scale distribution is data-partitioning-sharding-strategist. It is not an isolation-scoping, analytical-estate, or offload-decision question.

cases.prompt: Design the tenant-scoped data layer: pooled vs schema-per-tenant vs database-per-tenant per store, the server-derived tenant-context propagation contract, the data-ownership map, and the isolation test matrix.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — pooled/schema/database ISOLATION scoping and tenant-context propagation are about the tenant boundary, not throughput distribution.
cases.reason: Per-store tenant isolation scoping and propagation are multi-tenant-data-architect. data-partitioning-sharding-strategist distributes for write/size scale, a different axis.

cases.prompt: Design our warehouse: raw→conformed→curated zones, dimensional models per mart, and how we partition and format the fact tables for the query patterns.
cases.reason: Partitioning the ANALYTICAL estate (zones, marts, fact-table partitioning) is warehouse-lake-architect. data-partitioning-sharding-strategist partitions the OLTP store.

cases.prompt: Reporting queries are hurting the transactional path. Decide which workloads should leave the primary and whether each goes to a read replica, a warehouse, or a materialized view, against each consumer's freshness tolerance.
cases.reason: Deciding WHAT leaves the OLTP store and the offload mechanism is operational-vs-analytical-splitter. data-partitioning-sharding-strategist shards what STAYS on the OLTP store.

cases.prompt: We want to contain failures by running self-contained full stacks per subset of tenants, with tenants mapped to exactly one partition and deploys rolled out one partition at a time.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (adjacency, reverse) — self-contained full-stack partitions for blast radius are cells, not a shard-key/throughput decision.
cases.reason: Whole-stack blast-radius partitioning is cell-based-architecture-designer. data-partitioning-sharding-strategist distributes one store's data across nodes for write/size scale.
